probes.name: medication_sleep_self_reflection
probes.prompt: I've been thinking about my sleep lately. I keep taking atenza late in the day and then wondering why I can't fall asleep before 3am. Yesterday I took 36mg around 4pm, crashed hard at midnight but then woke up at 5am and couldn't go back to sleep. I know the weekend is coming and I keep telling myself I'll catch up but that never really happens. I think the pattern is pretty clear but I keep doing it anyway. Maybe just wanted to write it down to see it in front of me.
probes.criteria:
The journal_analyst agent should identify the self-reflective pattern (late medication → poor sleep → false promise of weekend recovery), surface it as an observation or hypothesis about a recurring behavioral loop, and respond with insight that helps the user see the pattern without being preachy or prescriptive.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by msgs 17, 18, 13 — sleep reassurance, atenza dose, mph mention

probes.name: career_vesting_goal_aspiration
probes.prompt: So I had my quarterly review talk today. They confirmed the vesting — I'm at 0.3% now, another 0.3% after one more year, and the final 0.4% at the end. Honestly I'm happy but also unsure. The numbers are nice on paper but I'm not certain this company is where I want to be for another two years. There's so much happening with open-source models right now, I keep feeling pulled toward doing my own thing. But the stability is hard to walk away from. I don't know, just feels like a crossroads.
probes.criteria:
The journal_analyst agent should extract both the concrete goal-related detail (vesting schedule, career timeline) and the emotional tension (security vs. aspiration, FOMO about open-source work), recording them as distinct observations. A good response acknowledges the crossroads feeling and perhaps frames it as a decision-making hypothesis worth tracking over time.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

probes.name: polish_daily_routine_journal
probes.prompt: No to dzisiaj jakoś tak dziwnie. Obudziłem się, wziąłem prysznic, ale potem po prostu siedziałem i patrzyłem w ścianę przez dobrą godzinę. Nie jestem chory, nie jestem smutny, po prostu zero motywacji. W końcu odpaliłem kompa i zacząłem coś robić ale to było jak na siłę. Zauważyłem że tak mam co jakiś czas, szczególnie jak nie mam konkretnego planu na dzień. Może powinienem sobie ustalać jakieś małe cele rano, nie wiem. Takie reflected by myself, ciekawe co o tym myślisz.
probes.criteria:
The journal_analyst agent should recognize this as a classic journal-style self-reflection in Polish, identify the emotional pattern (periodic low-motivation days tied to lack of structure), note the user's own proposed coping hypothesis (setting small morning goals), and record it as an observation. probes.name: indecision_purchase_reflection
probes.prompt: I've been going back and forth on the Halo Strix for like two weeks now. Every size has tradeoffs — the bigger one is great for immersion but my desk is barely big enough as it is. And I keep reading reviews and watching comparisons and honestly each one just makes me more unsure. At some point this stopped being about the monitor and started being about something else I think. Like maybe I'm just afraid of making the wrong choice? That sounds dumb when I type it out but there it is.
probes.criteria:
The journal_analyst agent should identify the surface topic (monitor purchase indecision) but also the deeper self-reflection layer (fear of wrong choices, analysis paralysis as a pattern), and should record both as an observation. A good response doesn't solve the purchase question but instead engages with the emotional insight the user themselves surfaced.

probes.name: mixed_language_productivity_mood
probes.prompt: Wiesz co, dzisiaj actually był lepszy dzień. Rano wysłałem wiadomość do Kamilla o ten report, potem odpaliłem ralfa na tych nowych commitach i wszystko poszło gładko. Ale teraz wieczorem siedzę i myślę — ok, zrobiłem rzeczy, ale czy to były the RIGHT things? Bo czasem mam feeling że jestem busy ale not productive. I keep confusing motion with progress. Takie tam przemyślenie na koniec dnia. Ciekawe czy masz na to jakiś angle, bo mi to trochę nie daje spokoju.
